slides.title: OpenID Connect Standard Claims Registration for CBOR Web Tokens

polls.text: Would you like to see the general text, as proposed (yes) or do you think that would be unacceptable (no; because you want a specific list of drafts instead)?

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: hello.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: we are all quic here

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Perhaps, "adapt" in this context means "make claim values or claim names consistent with the conventions of COSE / JOSE".

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: bijective, automatic translation between formats

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: could we perhaps scope this to the claims and identifiers in specific drafts, to avoid the unbounded scope?

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ^ we certainly could

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: We could limit the definition of claim values or claim names to specific drafts that the WG has reviews / support for. I would be fine with that.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: It sounds like @Mike Jones is not happy with that outcome.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: and the implication of that would be we can recharter to consider new claim names or claim values, and I would be fine with that as well.... perhaps I am more comfortable with the process of rechartering when the WG needs than other are.

chat.author: Christopher Inacio
chat.text: I can say I really wanted to say something like "define identifier via registry for other industrial registry as necessary to enable the claims per the SPICE objectives that allow consistent implementation of the SPICE data model"

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: To answer Roman's question, my belief is that SPICE has work to do to bring in claims that IETF people need for use elsewhere.  Yes, those are claims from elsewhere... and yes, they could it themselves, but we have work (perhaps not in this WG) that needs this data in this form.    And yeah, we also need a few good examples for people to emulate.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: and to be clear, there are DEs that manage JWT and CWT registries... Im sure they will appreciate a chartering process in front of batches of registrations, I again would be fine with that.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: to be clear, data: URIs can skip the base64 encoding piece

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: but you could join things with a character other than ":", maybe ":poop:"?

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: PHB basically said what I would have said... see also "Collision-Resistant Name" in RFC 7519

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: Is it true what @Rohan Mahy said?  I heard that they could be strings earlier.

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: domain names not being enduring is why they cannot be the authority of the URNs; they can be for URIs because they do not have the same requirement.

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: pets.com(as-of-2000)

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: There are ways around that (pwid for example uses time of retrieval and a specific archive for the identifier)

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: +1 to Ted... and ouch regarding your provisional comment, but good to know :)

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: tag:pets.com,2000: (RFC 4151)

chat.author: Christopher Inacio
chat.text: new naming schemes do need to come back to the WG

chat.author: Christopher Inacio
chat.text: just in case that wasn't already totally obvious to everyone

chat.author: Henk Birkholz
chat.text: Arch slide 5 : +1 it is minimum

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: privacy and security considerations of different flows seem necessary to me

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: privacy considerations would be helpful

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: damn, nick beat me to it

chat.author: Henk Birkholz
chat.text: Flow scenarios

chat.author: Henk Birkholz
chat.text: Not only "named edges"

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: RATS has flows for passport and background check.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9334#name-passport-model

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Maybe PQ stuff should be mentioned in the arch?

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: per martin's plea, I agree that feedback is important, and I'll try to take a look now

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: "dns names" like indieweb/Bluesky use of domain names as usernames?

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: User data is in fact already in the DNS, though not widely used.  See SMIMEA and OPENPGPKEY RRs

chat.author: Nick Doty
chat.text: I think it is possible that user acceptance of domain names has changed since Bluesky
